# db_migrate

> Dbmate-style async PostgreSQL migration runner. Single-file tool using asyncpg + structlog.

## What it does

Manages PostgreSQL schema migrations using plain SQL files with `-- migrate:up` and `-- migrate:down` markers. Tracks applied versions in a `schema_migrations` table. Each migration runs in a transaction.

## Commands

- `db_migrate.py` — apply all pending migrations
- `db_migrate.py --status` — show migration status
- `db_migrate.py --rollback` — rollback last migration
- `db_migrate.py --create "description"` — create new migration file

## Migration file format

Filename: `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description.sql`

```sql
-- migrate:up
CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);

-- migrate:down
DROP TABLE users;
```

## Configuration (env vars)

- `DATABASE_URL` (required) — PostgreSQL connection string
- `MIGRATIONS_DIR` (default: `db/migrations`) — path to migration files
- `SCHEMA_NAME` (default: `public`) — schema for tracking table

## Dependencies

asyncpg, structlog, python-dotenv

## Optional: llms-full.txt

For the full source code and API reference, see [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt).
